creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
(In reply to Cy Schubert from comment #5)

Sorry for the late response, time s a rare good ...

The port in question is built via poudriere on a building host running CURRENT (most recent with approximately one week delay counted from today, if this is usefull). The package we build is for 13.0-RELENG and 13.1-RELENG/RELEASE. Target systems (where the binaries will be used) are according to 13.0 or 13.1 boxes.
I suspected a critical configuration or critical compilation of a required port within one of the on 13.0 or 13.1 builder jails, since on 13.0, as long as the version number is not bumped up, the package building in poudriere might trigger a problem, but within the past days, 13.1 bumped the version numbers several times so the builder process of that strain starts again - but with the very same result as reported initially (tested yesterday, Friday ...).

There is no screen.core anywere.
There is no suspicious message in the log, not that I would stumble over it.
screen reports that it is terminating - so I guess there will no core dump or any crash footprint.

As described above, it is a package "home-brewn" via poudriere.

On any other host I/we install via make, not problems with the latest version. Installing the pkg from an official FreeBSD latest repository (4.9.0_5) does work as expected on both platforms 1^3.0 and 13.1.
